subtopic: UN80 Initiative and UN Core Organs
title: UN80 Initiative
focus: Launch, objectives, and work streams of the UN80 Initiative.
rationale: A specific recent initiative by the UN Secretary-General with clear structural goals and work streams, suitable for statement-based questions regarding its aims and coordinating body.
sectionExcerpt: Launched in March 2025 by the Secretary-General for the UN's 80th anniversary, the UN80 Initiative aims to streamline operations, improve efficiency, and reaffirm multilateralism. It features three work streams focusing on efficiencies, mandate implementation, and structural changes, coordinated by the UN80 Task Force.
evidenceBullets: Launched in March 2025 by the UN Secretary-General to mark the UN's 80th anniversary.
Aims to streamline operations, sharpen impact, and reassert the values of multilateralism.
Consists of three main work streams: identifying efficiencies, reviewing mandate implementation, and examining structural changes.
Coordinated by the UN80 Task Force, which is chaired by the Under-Secretary-General for Policy.

subtopic: UN80 Initiative and UN Core Organs
title: United Nations (UN) Core Structure and Agencies
focus: Genesis, main organs, specialized agencies, and membership rules of the UN.
rationale: Highly factual and traditionally examinable UPSC topic covering the 6 main organs, 15 specialized agencies and their headquarters, and membership admission rules.
sectionExcerpt: The UN was established in 1945 with its Charter signed in San Francisco. It comprises six main organs: UNGA, UNSC, ECOSOC, ICJ, Trusteeship Council (inactive since 1994), and the Secretariat. It has 193 members, with India as a founding member. Admission requires a UNGA decision upon UNSC recommendation. It also includes 15 specialized agencies with distinct headquarters.
evidenceBullets: Established in 1945 with the UN Charter signed in San Francisco; HQ is in New York.
Six main organs: UNGA, UNSC (15 members), ECOSOC (54 members), ICJ (The Hague), Trusteeship Council (inactive since 1994), and Secretariat.
Membership stands at 193 countries; India is a founding member.
States are admitted to membership by a decision of the General Assembly upon the recommendation of the Security Council.
15 Specialized Agencies include FAO (Rome), ILO (Geneva), IMF (Washington, DC), UNESCO (Paris), WHO (Geneva), and World Bank (Washington, DC).

subtopic: UN80 Initiative and UN Core Organs
title: UN Security Council (UNSC) Non-Permanent Members Election
focus: Recently elected non-permanent members of the UNSC.
rationale: A brief but specific current affairs update regarding the election of five new non-permanent members to the UNSC, testable as a direct factual question.
sectionExcerpt: Five countries, namely Bahrain, Colombia, the Democratic Republic of Congo, Latvia, and Liberia, were recently elected to serve as non-permanent members of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC).
evidenceBullets: Bahrain, Colombia, the Democratic Republic of Congo, Latvia, and Liberia were elected as non-permanent members of the UNSC.
